title: Pearson Edexcel International GCSE (9–1) Friday 14 November 2025 Biology Paper 2B

sub_questions.ms_text: Calculation: (17,000 / 30,000) * 100 = 56.66...% (1 mark). Final Answer: 57% (rounded to two significant figures) (1 mark).
Correct Answer: D (Plasmodium). Explanation: Amoeba, Chlorella, and Mucor are not parasites that cause malaria.
1. Adrenal glands release adrenaline (1); 2. Adrenaline increases heart rate / fight or flight response (1).
1. Muscles contract / stimulates contractions / stimulates peristalsis (1); 2. Moves faeces / waste through gut / colon / intestines (1).
1. Thicken / repair uterus lining / endometrium (1); 2. So embryo can implant / implantation can occur (1); 3. So placenta can grow into lining (1). (Maximum 2 marks)
Correct Answer: Mitosis. Explanation: Human body cell tumours grow through mitosis.

1. More air breathed in / more air passes to alveoli (1); 2. Maintains concentration gradient / steeper concentration gradient (1); 3. More oxygen diffuses (into blood) / faster diffusion (of oxygen) (1). (Maximum 2 marks)
1. Sucrose / sugar (1); 2. Amino acids (1). (Ignore glucose, starch, minerals, nutrients)

Investigate aphids and transpiration: 1. Use same species/size plants; 2. Control group: no aphids vs Experimental: aphids; 3. Measure mass change (before/after); 4. Leave for same time; 5. Control light/temp/humidity; 6. Repeat for averages. (Max 4 marks)

Effectiveness of P and Q: 1. Both reduce aphid numbers; 2. P reduces aphids more/faster initially; 3. Q is better for long-term (stays low); 4. P allows increase after 7-8 weeks (resistance/breakdown). (Max 4 marks)
Correct Answer: D (renal vein transporting blood away from the kidney). K is the renal vein.
Glucose concentration (W to X): 1. Selective reabsorption into blood; 2. Occurs in proximal convoluted tubule (PCT); 3. Via active transport. (Max 2 marks)
Urea concentration (X to Y): 1. Water is reabsorbed by osmosis; 2. In loop of Henle; 3. Urea is not reabsorbed (remains in filtrate). (Max 2 marks)

Dehydration effect on urea: 1. High blood concentration detected by hypothalamus; 2. Pituitary gland releases ADH; 3. Collecting duct becomes more permeable; 4. More water reabsorbed into blood; 5. Urea concentration in urine increases. (Max 4 marks)
Correct Answer: C (carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, and nitrogen). Enzymes are proteins and contain all four elements.
1. Temperature; 2. Potato age/variety; 3. Size/shape of slice; 4. Volume of buffer. (Max 2 marks)
Improving method: 1. Judging browning is subjective; 2. Use a colorimeter / standard color chart; 3. Smaller pH intervals; 4. Repeats for averages. (Max 2 marks)
pH effect on enzymes: 1. Optimum pH is 7; 2. Away from 7, enzyme denatures; 3. Active site shape changes; 4. Substrate (polyphenol) no longer fits. (Max 3 marks)
1. 9.4 (mean for mahogany); 2. 8 (range for mahogany).
Placing quadrats: 1. Use random number generator for coordinates; 2. Prevents bias; 3. Representative sample; 4. Control time of day/year. (Max 3 marks)
Indicator changes: 1. Jar A orange (CO2 equal); 2. Jar B yellow (CO2 increases); 3. In A, photosynthesis = respiration; 4. In B, respiration > photosynthesis. (Max 4 marks)
Protein synthesis: 1. Transcription in nucleus (mRNA made); 2. mRNA moves to ribosome; 3. tRNA brings amino acids; 4. Anticodons bind to codons; 5. Polypeptide chain formed. (Max 4 marks)
Calculation: ((250 - 20) / 20) * 100 = 1150%. Award 3 marks for 1150.

Edible vaccine evaluation: 1. Antibodies produced after 3 days; 2. Memory cells made; 3. Secondary response faster/bigger; 4. No needles (cheap/easy); 5. Small sample size/unreliable results. (Max 5 marks)
